commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f498d6c485862f3ec0547fe75675447fe557fe2d)
commands.review_summary:
The PR preserves Ollama tool-call IDs in the native stream conversion helpers, updates focused Ollama regression tests, and adds a changelog fix entry.

Reproducibility: yes. for the narrower conversion loss: current main source replaces inbound Ollama IDs and omits replay IDs, and the PR body includes before/after helper output. No for the live Gemini/Ollama Cloud 400, because no live provider trace or credentials-backed run was supplied.
commands.review_followup: Maintainers need to decide how native Ollama replay should preserve exact provider-owned IDs without regressing strict-ID compatibility, then require wrapped or live proof before merge.

target.title: fix(ollama): preserve tool call ids [AI-assisted]
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Gemini over native Ollama can return real tool call IDs while keeping the thought signature hidden; OpenClaw replaced those IDs with new `ollama_call_*` values and replayed assistant tool calls without `tool_calls[].id`.
- Solution: Preserve non-empty Ollama tool call IDs on ingest and include stored tool call IDs when replaying assistant turns back to Ollama.
- What changed: `extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts` now carries `tool_calls[].id` through both directions, with focused regression coverage for inbound, replay, parallel calls, and missing-ID fallback.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No provider routing, config, auth, model selection, tool-name normalization, argument parsing, or network behavior changed.

## Motivation

- Gemini over Ollama Cloud can reject follow-up turns with `400 Function call is missing a thought_signature` when the prior assistant tool call is replayed without the original Ollama tool call ID. Preserving that ID keeps the provider's hidden thought-signature handle intact.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #34008
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: native Ollama assistant tool call IDs were not preserved, so a Gemini/Ollama Cloud follow-up turn could lose the hidden thought-signature handle and fail on replay.
- Real environment tested: isolated OpenClaw checkout on the OpenClaw PR test server, using the real `extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts` conversion path and no secrets.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/ollama/src/stream-runtime.test.ts extensions/ollama/src/stream.test.ts
OPENCLAW_OLLAMA_ID_EXPECT=after node --import tsx ../ollama-tool-call-id-proof.mjs
```

- Evidence after fix:

```text
OPENCLAW Ollama tool-call id roundtrip proof
mode=after
storedIds=["fc_ollama_real_1","fc_ollama_real_2"]
replayIds=["fc_ollama_real_1","fc_ollama_real_2"]
fallbackId=ollama_call_08d634b9-3572-48a0-9c48-59ee5922a997
preservedIncoming=true
preservedReplay=true
fallbackGenerated=true
result=pass
```

- Observed result after fix: two real Ollama tool call IDs survive the assistant message conversion and are replayed as `tool_calls[].id`; responses without an ID still receive an `ollama_call_*` fallback.
- What was not tested: no live Ollama Cloud secret or real Gemini account was used in this PR run.
- Before evidence:

```text
OPENCLAW Ollama tool-call id roundtrip proof
mode=before
storedIds=["ollama_call_85de963d-bbb5-4fd2-8f89-7deea0311dcb","ollama_call_9cd03228-3a87-48a6-82ba-bed2216c86a6"]
replayIds=[null,null]
fallbackId=ollama_call_ee91a659-4811-4754-8618-12618b413bc8
preservedIncoming=false
preservedReplay=false
fallbackGenerated=true
result=fail
```

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: the native Ollama adapter treated incoming tool call IDs as disposable and generated fresh local IDs, then omitted IDs when converting stored assistant tool calls back into Ollama chat messages.
- Missing detection / guardrail: tests covered tool names and argument normalization but not Ollama tool call ID roundtrip behavior.
- Contributing context (if known): Gemini/Ollama Cloud may use the returned tool call ID as an opaque handle for a hidden thought signature when the explicit signature is not surfaced.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/ollama/src/stream-runtime.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: inbound Ollama tool call IDs are preserved, parallel IDs remain distinct, stored assistant tool call IDs are replayed to Ollama, and missing IDs still get the existing fallback.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug lives entirely in the native Ollama message conversion layer.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing Ollama conversion tests covered tool calls but intentionally did not assert IDs before this PR.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Native Ollama Gemini tool-call conversations should continue across tool-result turns instead of failing because the original tool call ID was lost. Other Ollama tool-call behavior is unchanged.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
Ollama tool_call.id -> OpenClaw ollama_call_<uuid> -> replay without id -> Gemini/Ollama loses hidden signature handle

After:
Ollama tool_call.id -> OpenClaw stores same id -> replay with same id -> provider can match the tool-result turn
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux test server
- Runtime/container: Node 22.22.2, isolated OpenClaw checkout
- Model/provider: native Ollama path, simulated Gemini/Ollama Cloud tool call IDs
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Convert an Ollama response containing two tool calls with IDs through `buildAssistantMessage`.
2. Replay the stored assistant content through `convertToOllamaMessages`.
3. Confirm both IDs are preserved in storage and replay.
4. Repeat without an incoming ID and confirm the existing `ollama_call_*` fallback remains.

### Expected

- Stored IDs and replay IDs equal the real Ollama IDs.
- Missing incoming IDs still generate `ollama_call_*`.

### Actual

- Before the patch, stored IDs were random and replay IDs were absent.
- After the patch, stored and replay IDs are preserved.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/ollama/src/stream-runtime.test.ts extensions/ollama/src/stream.test.ts
2 files / 90 tests passed
```

```text
git diff --check
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts extensions/ollama/src/stream-runtime.test.ts
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts extensions/ollama/src/stream-runtime.test.ts
```

All passed.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: inbound ID preservation, outbound ID replay, parallel tool call IDs, and fallback ID generation when Ollama does not send an ID.
- Edge cases checked: provider-prefixed tool names still normalize; stringified arguments still deserialize; unsafe integer parsing remains covered by existing tests updated for IDs.
- What you did **not** verify: live Ollama Cloud credentials or a real Gemini account.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Some Ollama-compatible provider could send a blank or non-string ID.
  - Mitigation: only non-empty string IDs are preserved; existing `ollama_call_*` fallback remains for missing/invalid IDs.
target.author: iwhatsskill

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
